course_description:
This course considers the nature of the law of tort. It examines intentional torts against the person, and torts in relation to land and the enjoyment of land. The course also includes an introduction to the tort of negligence (including modification by the Civil Liability Act 2003 (Qld)).

Law of Torts I introduces students to the law of torts. The law of torts consists of the law of civil (non-criminal) wrongs other than breach of contract. Law of Torts I covers the following: the nature and aims of tort law; intentional torts against the person; torts to land (trespass and nuisance);ﾠbasic elements of the law of negligence, as modified by the Civil Liability Act 2003 (Qld); damages for personal injury; and actions on death.ﾠ

course_aims:
Teaching Mission Statement

The mission of the School of Law is to educate students in and about the law. We seek to assist you to think critically about the law and to investigate its structures, principles, policies, and values. This is achieved by engaging with you as fellow investigators in a range of critical and intellectual conversations about the law and its place in society. Our role is thus to guide you in your own intellectual journey in the law and to equip you with the necessary skills that will enable you to continue that journey long after you have left the School.

Course Aims

The broad aims of the course are:

learning_outcomes.description: Understand and articulate the key concepts and principles in the law of torts.
Engage thoughtfully with case law and statutes.
Analyse facts and identify legal solutions to the problems raised by those facts.
Undertake legal research in relation to case law and legislation.

assessment_details.task_description: Students will be required to undertake a closed book, in-semester exam. Students will have 90 minutes working time. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 12 (24 Feb - 25 May)
learning_activities.topic: Lectures The lecture topics are: Torts: An Overview/Intentional Torts I Intentional Torts Against the Person Problem Solvi Trespass to Land Nuisance Negligence 1- The Duty of Care Negligence 2 - Breach of Duty Negligence 3 - Causation and Remoteness of Damage Negligence 4 - Multiple Tortfeasors Negligence 5 - Defences Actions on Death Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 11 (24 Feb - 18 May)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorials The tutorial topics are: Torts: An Overview Intentional Torts Against the Person Problem Solvi Intentional Torts Against the Person False Imprisonment - Case Reading Exercise Negligence Overview Negligence 2 - Breach of Duty Negligence 3 - Causation and Remoteness of Damage Negligence 4 - Multiple Tortfeasors Negligence 5 - Defences Revision Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
